steps.title: Identify Moore's philosophical foundation
steps.content: The key signal is Moore's core claim that all people instinctively know certain things to be true—having a body, living in a world with objects—regardless of what they philosophically profess.

steps.title: Apply Moore's logic to skepticism
steps.content: Choice D points out that skepticism contradicts unavoidable instinctive beliefs about bodies and the world that Moore identifies. Choices A and B misrepresent the author's stance toward Moore (either as agreeing with critics or treating instinct as a barrier); C wrongly treats skeptics' instinctive proof-rejection as equally valid. That matches choice D — D is correct.
